eliminated_candidates.Graphene: G band (~1580 cm⁻¹) and 2D band (~2650–2700 cm⁻¹) are outside the scan range (100–1498 cm⁻¹); cannot confirm graphene without both diagnostic peaks.
eliminated_candidates.hBN: hBN E₂g window shows only 0.2% signal at 1366 cm⁻¹ (weak); no strong hBN peak present. Additionally, STRONG peaks at ~380 and ~406 cm⁻¹ rule out hBN.
eliminated_candidates.MoS2: Not eliminated — STRONG E²₁g peak at 380 cm⁻¹ (100%) and STRONG A₁g peak at 406 cm⁻¹ (35.4%) are both present with ~26 cm⁻¹ separation, fully consistent with MoS₂.
eliminated_candidates.WSe2: WSe₂ A₁g/E²₁g window at ~242 cm⁻¹ shows only 0.4% (weak); absence of any signal in 238–272 cm⁻¹ window rules out WSe₂. Also, STRONG peaks at ~380 and ~406 cm⁻¹ exceed the WSe₂ primary peak and rule out WSe₂.
eliminated_candidates.Ti3C2Tx: Peak/mean ratio of 58.5 far exceeds the threshold of 5, indicating a sharp crystalline peak is present. STRONG signal at ~380 cm⁻¹ in the MoS₂ E²₁g window rules out Ti₃C₂Tₓ.

key_evidence: MoS₂ E²₁g / E′ in-plane mode at 380 cm⁻¹: 100% of diagnostic_max (STRONG)
MoS₂ A₁g / A₁′ out-of-plane mode at 406 cm⁻¹: 35.4% of diagnostic_max (STRONG)
Peak separation of ~26 cm⁻¹ between E²₁g and A₁g is within the expected 18–25 cm⁻¹ range for MoS₂ (slightly above, consistent with bulk or few-layer)
All other candidate primary windows show weak or absent signals (<8%)
reasoning: The spectrum is dominated by two STRONG peaks at 380 cm⁻¹ and 406 cm⁻¹, corresponding to the MoS₂ E²₁g (in-plane) and A₁g (out-of-plane) modes with a separation of ~26 cm⁻¹, which is characteristic of few-layer or bulk MoS₂. No other candidate material produces both of these peaks simultaneously: WSe₂ lacks any signal in its 238–272 cm⁻¹ primary window, hBN and graphene show no relevant signals, and the TMC flatness criterion is decisively violated by the sharp crystalline peaks. The scan range (100–1498 cm⁻¹) excludes the graphene G and 2D windows, but the presence of the two MoS₂ characteristic peaks with correct positions and separation provides high confidence in the MoS₂ classification.
